body.title: [hold] Pool semantics: max_instances (singleton = pool-of-1), .0 durable / 1+ ephemeral wiped on startup
body.intention: I intend that a worker class caps its pool at max_instances (singleton = 1) and that instance .0 is durable while 1+ wipe their folder on startup — so a singleton needs no special-casing.
body.description:
**Blocked on: T-shim-lease-instance-numbers** (extends the free-list with a cap + wipe rule). Homogeneity: a singleton is just max_instances=1.

**What to build:**
- A `max_instances` property on the device/class (default e.g. a sensible N; singletons set 1). The class-shim REFUSES to allocate beyond max_instances (returns no slot / the caller queues or Granny gets 'no capacity').
- STARTUP WIPE: instance .0 NEVER wipes its folder (durable foreground/primary); instances 1+ WIPE their assigned ~/.unseen_university/devices/<ABBREV>.<N>/ folder on startup (clean slate).

**Completion criteria:** a class with max_instances=1 (singleton, e.g. Granny) allocates DS.0/Granny.0 and REFUSES a second; a class with max_instances=3 allows .0-.2 and refuses .3; on startup DS.1's folder is wiped, DS.0's is preserved.

**Test plan (real red->green):** unit — set max_instances=1, assert the 2nd allocate is refused; set 3, assert the 4th refused; assert startup wipes DS.1's folder but not DS.0's. Red before the cap/wipe exist; green after.

**Validation (runtime):** Granny (singleton) only ever runs Granny.0; a restarted DS.1 comes up with an empty folder while DS.0 retains prior state. Observable via allocation-refused log + folder contents after startup.

**Logging:** log.info('pool: max_instances=%d reached for <class>, allocation refused') ; log.info('startup: wiped %s (ephemeral)', instance_name) — skipped for .0.

**Observable via:** log.warning('pool: no capacity for <class> (max_instances=1)') + DS.1 folder empty post-startup, DS.0 folder intact.

**Affected files (indicative):** unseen_university/devices/<worker>/shim.py (cap in allocate), device.py (max_instances property), instance startup (folder wipe).

**Rollback:** git revert — removes the cap (unbounded pool) + the wipe. No migration.

**Scope boundary:** the cap + wipe only; leasing mechanics are T-shim-lease-instance-numbers.
